old_claim.note: Railroads provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Earlier military technical academies established the institutional foundation for professional officer education before staff colleges added a selective mid-career tier for command and staff work.

source_shape.source_locator: Journal pp. 124-125, sections "Phase One - Origins" and "Phase Two - the Kriegsakademie Era": military academies established the foundation for professional military education, followed by a distinct mid-career staff-college tier in the early nineteenth century.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The historical survey identifies earlier military academies as the foundation of professional military education and staff colleges as the subsequent mid-career tier.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The local artillery_fortification_schools node represents the documented earlier institutional layer of military technical education. Public railways began after the 1810 staff-college anchor and therefore cannot define the founding lineage.

ontology_before: military_staff_colleges treated public railways as a direct enabling prerequisite and used a railway-history page as both node and edge evidence.
ontology_after: military_staff_colleges now follows the documented educational lineage from earlier military technical schools to a selective mid-career staff-college tier; railways are later subjects of staff planning, not a founding dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-military-staff-college-scope: artillery_fortification_schools is a historical predecessor and railroads is not a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A reliable military-education history demonstrates that the scoped staff-college tier first emerged from railway institutions rather than from earlier professional military schools.
The local artillery_fortification_schools node is respecified so narrowly that it no longer represents institutional military technical education preceding staff colleges.
short_reason: Staff colleges developed as a new tier of professional military education; the railway citation described transport history and postdated the institution.
